questions.question: What does 'type narrowing' mean in TypeScript?
options: A) Converting a value to a different type at runtime
B) Refining a broader type to a more specific type within a code block
C) Removing properties from an object type
D) Making a type alias shorter
C) Error: toFixed is not a function

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a valid typeof guard return value in JavaScript?
What does the 'instanceof' operator check?
options: A) Whether a value matches a TypeScript interface
B) Whether an object's prototype chain contains a specific constructor
C) Whether a type is a subtype of another
D) Whether two objects have the same properties

questions.question: How does the 'in' operator narrow types?
options: A) It checks if a value is inside an array
B) It checks if a property exists on an object, narrowing the union
C) It checks if a type is defined in a module
D) It iterates over an object's keys

questions.question: What is a discriminated union in TypeScript?
options: A) A union of types that share a common literal-typed property used for narrowing
B) A union that excludes certain types
C) A union of only primitive types
D) A union that can only contain two members

questions.question: What is the return type annotation for a custom type guard function?

options: A) It converts val to a string
B) It tells TypeScript that if the function returns true, val is a string
C) It throws an error if val is not a string
D) It creates a new string from val

questions.question: What is the purpose of the assertNever pattern?
options: A) To assert that a value is always truthy
B) To ensure all cases of a discriminated union are handled exhaustively
C) To prevent a function from ever being called
D) To mark code as unreachable for performance

questions.question: What happens if you add a new variant to a discriminated union but forget to handle it in a switch, with assertNever in the default?
options: A) Nothing, the code works fine
C) A compile-time error occurs because the new variant can't be assigned to never
D) TypeScript ignores the new variant

questions.question: What does typeof null return in JavaScript?
What is an assertion function in TypeScript?
options: A) A function that returns a boolean
B) A function with 'asserts param is Type' return type that throws if the condition is false
C) A function that converts types
D) A function that always returns true

questions.question:
Which syntax declares an assertion function?

function assertIsString(val: unknown): ???
Why can't 'instanceof' be used with TypeScript interfaces?
options: B) Interfaces don't exist at runtime, so there's no constructor to check
C) instanceof only works with primitive types
D) Interfaces can't be part of unions

questions.question: Which narrowing technique works best for distinguishing interface types that share no common properties?
options: C) The 'in' operator checking for a unique property

questions.question: What is the 'never' type used for in exhaustive checks?
options: B) Representing a value that should never occur if all cases are handled
